evals.prompt: how many photos were uploaded this week?
evals.expected_output: Concise report with verdict-first structure, specific upload counts from Supabase, saved to docs/analysis/ with timestamp
assertions: Report starts with a one-sentence verdict as the first line (no preamble, no 'Great question', no 'I analyzed')
Report is saved to docs/analysis/ with a timestamped filename
Every Why bullet includes a specific number backed by data (not vague claims)
Report includes a Data sources section

evals.prompt: which tools are staff using most?
evals.expected_output: Report explains Statsig data gap with root cause (environment misconfiguration), saved to docs/analysis/ with timestamp, includes fix steps
assertions: Report starts with a one-sentence verdict as the first line (no preamble)
Report is saved to docs/analysis/ with a timestamped filename
Report explains the root cause of missing data (Statsig environment misconfiguration) rather than just reporting 0 events
Report includes actionable next steps (e.g., how to fix the environment)

evals.prompt: how many reservations do we have this week vs last week?
evals.expected_output: Verdict-first report comparing this week vs last week with specific numbers, efficient single SQL query, saved to docs/analysis/
assertions: Report starts with a one-sentence verdict as the first line (no preamble)
Report is saved to docs/analysis/ with a timestamped filename
Report compares this week vs last week with specific numbers for both periods
Data is fetched with 1-3 SQL queries (not 4+)

evals.prompt: Our signups dropped 60% last week compared to the week before. What happened? We use Mixpanel.
evals.expected_output: Should use parallel investigation agents (data quality, seasonality, product changes), run a skeptic challenge on findings, and produce a rigorous report with confidence tags.
assertions: Spawns parallel investigation subagents (data quality, seasonality, product/external) rather than investigating sequentially
Runs a skeptic challenge agent on the synthesized findings before finalizing the report
Considers data quality as a possible explanation before concluding the drop is real
Includes confidence tags (High/Medium/Low) on findings
Does not present the 60% drop as a confirmed real behavioral change without evidence
Asks for Mixpanel credentials or uses existing ones to investigate

evals.prompt: How is our onboarding funnel performing? We use Mixpanel.
evals.expected_output: Should frame sub-questions, ask for Mixpanel credentials, structure a funnel analysis plan, and include rigor checks in the approach.
assertions: Frames the question into 2+ specific measurable sub-questions before fetching data
Asks for Mixpanel credentials (service account username and secret)
Plans to use breakdown queries rather than per-value filters
Includes data quality validation in the analysis approach

evals.prompt: Our daily active users dropped 35% this weekend vs Friday. Is something broken? We use Mixpanel.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize seasonality, spawn parallel agents to investigate, and not alarm the user.
assertions: Recognizes the weekday-vs-weekend pattern as likely seasonality, not a real problem
Does not alarm the user or treat the 35% drop as a confirmed issue
Suggests comparing weekend-to-weekend (same day-of-week) instead of weekend-to-weekday
Mentions that 30-60% weekend drops are typical for B2B products or similar context
Uses parallel investigation path (Path B) since this is an investigative question about explaining a change

evals.prompt: Our checkout conversion rate dropped from 12% to 4% overnight two days ago. Revenue is down 65%. We need to figure out what happened ASAP. We use Mixpanel.
evals.expected_output: Should spawn all 3 parallel investigators + skeptic. Must check data quality first (is the checkout event still firing?), check for deploys, and not jump to a behavioral conclusion. The skeptic should challenge any premature conclusions.
assertions: Spawns 3 parallel investigation agents simultaneously (not sequentially)
Data quality agent checks if checkout/payment events are still firing correctly
Product changes agent checks git history for recent deploys around the timing of the drop
Runs a skeptic challenge on findings before presenting the final report
Addresses or acknowledges skeptic challenges in the final report
Asks for Mixpanel credentials to actually investigate
